title: salix × laschiana
intro: Salix × laschiana, also known as hybrid willow or white willow, is a hybrid species of the Salix genus, combining the characteristics of white willow (Salix alba) and gooley willow (Salix fragilis). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × laschiana include relief from pain, reduction of fever, and alleviation of rheumatic and arthritic symptoms. The herbal preparations of Salix × laschiana are typically made from its bark, which can be dried, powdered, or infused in hot water to create a tea, and it is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
